name: Checkout.com
firstSuccessOutcome: A test card payment completes through Flow in the sandbox and appears on the Dashboard Payments page.

steps.action: Open Checkout.com docs Get started guide
Create a sandbox test account
Sign in to the Checkout.com Dashboard
Create public and secret API keys
Install checkout-web-components package via npm
Create a Payment Session server-side with secret key
Initialize and mount Flow with public key
Pay with test card 4242 in Flow
Confirm payment status on Dashboard Payments page

name: GoCardless
routeName: Send your first API request (Sandbox Billing Request)
firstSuccessOutcome: A test billing request is authorised in Sandbox, creating your first customer and payment mandate.

steps.action: Open GoCardless developer getting-started guide
Sign up for a GoCardless Sandbox account
Sign in to the Sandbox dashboard
Create a read-write access token under Developers
POST /billing_requests with a mandate_request
POST /billing_request_flows to get authorisation_url
Open authorisation_url, enter test bank details
See new customer on dashboard customers page

name: Mollie
firstSuccessOutcome: A test-mode payment is created via the API, returning a 201 with a hosted checkout URL.

steps.action: Open Mollie API authentication getting-started docs
Create a Mollie account via the Web App
Verify your email address
Create a website profile in the dashboard
Copy the Test API key under Developers
POST /v2/payments with amount and redirectUrl
Receive 201 with checkout URL in response

name: Paddle
routeName: Paddle.js sandbox quickstart (open checkout, test payment)
firstSuccessOutcome: A test payment completes through an overlay checkout and appears in the sandbox Transactions.

steps.action: Open Paddle developer quickstart docs
Sign up for a Paddle sandbox account
Confirm your email address
Create a product and price in dashboard
Create a client-side token under Authentication
Include Paddle.js via CDN script tag
Initialize Paddle.js with sandbox token
Open checkout with your price ID
Complete checkout using Paddle test card
See test payment in sandbox Transactions

name: Harness
routeName: Get Started with Harness CI (first pipeline on Harness Cloud)
firstSuccessOutcome: Your first CI pipeline runs a build successfully on Harness Cloud infrastructure.

steps.action: Open Harness CI get-started onboarding guide
Sign up for a free Harness account
Log in; default org and project created
Open Continuous Integration module, create a pipeline
Add a Build stage to the pipeline
Choose Harness Cloud build infrastructure
Add a Run step with build commands
Select Run, then Run Pipeline
Watch the build stage complete successfully

name: JFrog
routeName: Artifactory Cloud free trial + Quick Repository Setup
firstSuccessOutcome: Your first repository is created in a hosted JFrog Artifactory Cloud instance.

steps.action: Open the JFrog start-free trial page
Start a 14-day Cloud trial, no card
Verify email and set instance password
Wait for your Cloud instance to provision
Log in at your company.jfrog.io URL
Open Quick Repository Creation from login menu
Select a package type and create repositories
See new repository in the repository list

name: Sonatype
routeName: Nexus Repository Community Edition self-hosted quickstart
firstSuccessOutcome: Nexus Repository Community Edition runs locally and you create your first repository.

steps.action: Open Sonatype Nexus Repository download page
Fill form to download Community Edition
Extract archive; ensure Java runtime installed
Run ./nexus run to start the server
Open http://localhost:8081 in your browser
Sign in as admin; complete onboarding wizard
Create a repository via Create repository
See your new repository in the list

name: Spacelift
routeName: Quick Start (first stack from Terraform starter repo)
firstSuccessOutcome: Your first Spacelift stack run plans and applies infrastructure from the starter repository.

steps.action: Open the Spacelift Quick Start docs
Create account via GitHub, Google, or GitLab
Choose an account name and log in
Integrate source code using Terraform starter repo
Create your first stack from the LaunchPad
Open the stack and click Trigger
Click Confirm to apply the changes
Wait for the run to finish successfully

name: SUSE
routeName: Rancher Developer Access trial + Rancher Desktop local cluster
firstSuccessOutcome: Rancher Desktop provisions a local K3s Kubernetes cluster you can reach with kubectl.

steps.action: Open SUSE Rancher Developer Access page and docs
Create a SUSE account, start free trial
Log in to SUSE Customer Center
Activate subscription with emailed registration code
Download and install Rancher Desktop
Choose container engine and enable Kubernetes
Rancher Desktop provisions a local K3s cluster
Run kubectl get nodes to confirm cluster
